description: Rigby surfaced scheduled task format using raw cron expressions, multi-line descriptions with excessive label padding. David asked for plain-English timing, not implementation details. Output was formatted for a sysadmin, not an executive.
correction: When presenting scheduling proposals, use plain English timing (e.g., 'Weekdays at 6:00 AM') not cron syntax. Keep descriptions single-line. Don't pad label:value pairs with extra spaces. Better yet — just create the tasks directly instead of previewing config format.
systemic_fix: All agent output to David should be executive-grade, not technical. If a tool can be invoked directly, invoke it — don't show the configuration schema as a preview.
